assumptions: The developer has a supported browser, email address, and a machine that can install Node.js 20+ and npm.
No HubSpot account, Personal Access Key, or local HubSpot CLI config exists yet.
The developer intends to use HubSpot's current developer-platform Quickstart rather than a Marketplace-published production app.

developer_goal: Create a HubSpot account and reach the Quickstart first success of deploying and previewing the boilerplate Get Started App Card on a contact record.

first_success.outcome: The boilerplate app is built, deployed, installed, assigned to the default contact record view, and visibly rendered while local development is running.
first_success.observable_signal: A contact's Overview tab displays Get Started App Card with the Developing locally badge while hs project dev is running.

route_selection.surface: HubSpot developer-platform Quickstart with HubSpot CLI
route_selection.rule: Prefer the documented hs get-started Quickstart over the longer hs project create setup guide when both reach a first installed app card.

candidates.name: Quickstart via hs get-started on a free standard HubSpot account
candidates.reason: Official recommended path, but account-signup fields and Developer tools permission granting remain incompletely evidenced from public docs.

candidates.name: Full create-an-app flow via hs project create with OAuth or static token choices
candidates.reason: HubSpot documents this as the longer customization path after pointing newcomers to the Quickstart.

candidates.name: Install and test only inside a developer test account after standard-account setup
candidates.reason: The Quickstart can install into the authenticated account or prompt for a test/sandbox account; which path is required for first success is not fixed.

required_path.action: Create a HubSpot account if one is not already available.

required_fields.label: HubSpot account signup fields
required_fields.notes: Quickstart links to account creation but does not enumerate the hosted signup form.

required_path.observable_result: The developer has an authenticated standard HubSpot account that can use developer tools.

required_path.action: Install the HubSpot CLI globally and authenticate with hs account auth.
required_path.observable_result: The CLI stores account credentials in the local HubSpot CLI config.

required_path.action: Run hs get-started, select App, name the project, and upload it to initialize the first build and deploy.
required_path.observable_result: HubSpot builds and deploys the boilerplate project.

required_path.action: Install the unverified app, connect it, and assign Get Started App Card to the default contact record view.

required_fields.label: Get Started App Card location checkbox

required_path.observable_result: The app card is configured on the default contact Overview middle column.

required_path.action: Run hs project dev, select the testing account when prompted, and open a contact Overview tab.
required_path.observable_result: Get Started App Card appears with the Developing locally badge.

prerequisites.description: Node.js 20 or later and npm are required to install and run the HubSpot CLI.
Personal Access Key scopes are limited by the HubSpot user's permissions, including Developer tools access for project upload.

external_gates.description: A user without Developer tools access can create a Personal Access Key that still cannot upload developer projects.
Installing an unverified app requires an explicit confirmation checkbox before Connect App.

unavoidable_waits.description: HubSpot must complete the first project build and deploy before install and card setup continue.

platform_outcomes.description: HubSpot creates and deploys the boilerplate project after hs get-started upload.
Local hs project dev serves the card with a Developing locally badge on the contact record.

excluded.item: Reading HubSpot conceptual docs beyond the Quickstart
excluded.reason: Documentation reading is not a product action.

excluded.item: Full hs project create customization with distribution and auth feature prompts
excluded.reason: HubSpot presents this as the longer path after the Quickstart.

sources.title: HubSpot Account Types
evidence_supported: Developer test account creation from a standard account

sources.title: Authenticate the CLI with personal access keys
evidence_supported: Personal Access Key scopes inherit user permissions
Developer tools access required to upload projects

uncertainties.question: What exact forms, verification steps, and required fields create a new standard HubSpot account from a cold start?
uncertainties.impact: Signup burden sits before CLI setup and cannot be counted without a public signup-field inventory.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: A dated walkthrough of the current HubSpot account signup URL linked from the Quickstart, listing every required field and verification gate.

uncertainties.question: What administrator or permission flow grants Developer tools access so a Personal Access Key can upload projects?
uncertainties.impact: Without that grant, authenticated CLI users still fail at project upload.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: Official documentation or a product walkthrough that shows how Developer tools access is assigned in a fresh free account versus an existing team account.

uncertainties.question: Must first success use the standard account, a newly created developer test account, or either?
uncertainties.impact: Test-account creation adds actions and may change which install path is shortest.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: Confirm whether the Quickstart's Developing locally terminal is reachable by installing only into the authenticated standard account.
